title: Example Maps of Exposure, Sensitivity, and Vulnerability for Selected Social-Ecological Hazards in the Krakow Metropolitan Area
author: Benati, Giulia
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
This dataset provides example maps of exposure, sensitivity, and resulting vulnerability for two social-ecological hazards— lack of recreational opportunities  and  air pollution —in the Metropolitan Area of Krakow (Poland). These maps were produced as part of a broader spatial vulnerability assessment aimed at supporting the prioritization of Nature-Based Solutions (NBS) within the INTERLACE project. 
 Each theme is represented by: 
 
 
 One  sensitivity map  showing population or ecological groups affected; 
 
 
 One  exposure map  showing the spatial distribution of environmental pressures; 
 
 
 One  vulnerability map  integrating the two dimensions. 
 
 
 The maps are provided in .jpg format for illustrative purposes. All spatial processing was carried out in QGIS and ArcGIS, based on publicly available or institutional geodata. The full methodology, data sources, and indicator definitions are described in the associated mapping protocol (DOI:  10.5281/zenodo.14624072 ). 
 These files are intended as  visual examples  to complement the vulnerability mapping framework presented in the manuscript and should not be interpreted as final decision-support tools. For detailed metadata and processing steps, please refer to the mapping protocol.
